free_text: Spallation of Cu by 3.9-GeV N-14 ions and 3.9-GeV
protons
Gamma-ray abundances,
(GRA) except where indicated.
GRA for Cl-34m.
GRA for K-43.
GRA for K-44.
GRA for Cr-49.
GRA for Fe-53.
GRA for Co-62.
GRA for Cu-60.
GRA for Zn-63.
Beta abundance data were extracted from 6-th edition of
Table of Isotopes and Nuclear Data/B1-B3.
Mass Yield Curve was Adopted As Form
Y(A)=X3+X4(A-50), Where
Experimental Data were Fitted by Polynomial
Form of (ZP-Z).
Most Probable Charge of Reaction Products:
For Cu-0(N-14,X)
28.28 +- 0.02    from Cu-61/Co-61 Ratio
26.21 +- 0.01    from Co-56/Mn-56 Ratio
22.55 +- 0.02    from Cr-48/Sc-48 Ratio
For Cu-0(P,X)
28.20 +- 0.01    from Cu-61/Co-61 Ratio
26.14 +- 0.01    from Co-56/Mn/56 Ratio
22.5  +- 0.01    from Cr-48/Sc-48 Ratio
20.21 +- 0.01    from Sc-43/K-43  Ratio
Approx. 70 cm**3 Ge(Li) Detector [resolution
approx. 1.9 keV at 1332 keV].
N-14 Ion Beam of the Princeton Particle
Accelerator.
The Proton Beam of the Brookhaven
Alternating Gradient Synchrotron.
/by Authors/ The basic data from present experiment
consisted of a large set of counting rates at the ends
of the irradiations for the various radiations of the
isotopes of interest. there were converted to
disintegration rates by application of corrections for
detector efficiency ,including summing corrections,
and radiation abundance and finally relative or
absolute cross sections were derived which included
corrections for temporal variations of the beam.
Standard deviations assigned to the individual
measurements of cross sections included, in addition to
the nominal statistical errors from spectral and decay
curve resolutions, the following:
Uncertainty of chemical yield determinations;
A contribution for reproducibility of the beta
counts;
A contribution for reproducibility of the gamma
counts.
Tbl. I from Phys.Rev.,C10(1974)739
Decay-Flag has been saved in O0542002 only.
Last checking has been done.
SD: Corrections in all Subents. In case of
cosmetic corrections in the Subents HISTORY code wasn't
added.
Abundance of 1157.-keV quantum is given For equilibrium
with Sc-44g.
B+ abundance is given
for equilibrium Zn-62 + Cu-62.
- By Authors - The represented value are mean
saturation disintegration rates (1/min) for irradiation
conditions divided by 1000.
Three copper targets, each target consisted of three
discs of high-purity copper [1.6 cm in diam.,
345 mg/cm**2 thick] which were held together by a
wrapping of 10.5-mg/cm**2 copper. The thickness of
these targets was selected as a compromise between
activity production and energy loss of the beam.
After irradiation two foils were flown to Brookhaven,
third was retained at Prinseton.
The result of a single determination.
the agreement between duplicates was poorer than
expected.
see decay-data for Al-28 too.
the result of a single determination. The value is
suspect. See appendix in the original paper.
SD: DECAY-DATA corrected in several cases
of beta decay (comma was missed). Cosmetic corrections.
